name: Oña — circular from Poza de la Sal
name_original: Oña — circular desde Poza de la Sal
region_intro: Touring cycling around Poza De La Sal, nestled in the province of Burgos, Spain, offers a diverse landscape for exploration. The region is characterized by unique geological formations, including ancient salt flats situated within a striking diapir, which provides insight into geological history. To the north, the Montes Obarenes form a picturesque backdrop, while the terrain descends to the expansive La Bureba Plain. This varied topography creates a rich environment for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Poza De La Sal The most popular touring cycling route is Oña — circular from Poza de la Sal, a 45.8 miles (73.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 18 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Poza de la Sal – Red Rock Valley loop from Poza de la Sal, a difficult 44.2 miles (71.1 km) path. This route offers expansive views and challenging ascents through the Red Rock Valley. Local touring cyclists also love the Town Hall Arch – Oña Historic Town Center loop from Poza de la Sal, a 17.2 miles (27.6 km) trail leading through the historic town center, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Touring cycling around Poza De La Sal is defined by ancient salt flats, the La Bureba Plain, and the distant Montes Obarenes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult, longer routes. The routes in Poza De La Sal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from nearly 30 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Poza De La Sal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Poza de la Sal?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Poza de la Sal, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Poza de la Sal?
A: The terrain around Poza de la Sal is quite varied. You'll encounter the unique geological formations of the ancient salt flats, the expansive La Bureba Plain, and the more challenging ascents and descents of the Montes Obarenes to the north. Routes can range from gentle slopes, especially on sections of the Vía Verde, to significant elevation gains in mountainous areas.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly touring cycling routes?
A: While many routes feature challenging ascents, the region also offers easier options. The Vía Verde (Greenway) is particularly noted for its gentle slopes, making it ideal for touring cyclists seeking a less strenuous ride. This route follows old railway lines, providing a smooth and enjoyable experience.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Poza de la Sal are designed as circular loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027992313/ona-circular-from-poza-de-la-sal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña — circular from Poza de la Sal&lt;/a&gt; offers a longer, challenging loop, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31483030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall Arch – Oña Historic Town Center loop from Poza de la Sal&lt;/a&gt; provides a moderate, shorter option.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling?
A: You can explore a wealth of natural and historical sites. The ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4405981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Rock Valley&lt;/a&gt; and the unique salt flats are prominent features. You might also encounter geological formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5295093" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elephant Rock&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6676863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CascadaDeLaHuevera&lt;/a&gt; waterfall. The region's varied topography, including the Montes Obarenes and La Bureba Plain, offers diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical sites or towns to explore on a touring bike?
A: Absolutely. Poza de la Sal itself is a historic fortified town with a medieval character, including the 14th-century Rojas castle. Many routes lead to or through other historic areas, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Center&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2390054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Town Center&lt;/a&gt;, where you can find ancient churches and charming streets. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4988248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santander–Mediterranean Greenway&lt;/a&gt; also passes by old train stations, offering a glimpse into the past.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Poza de la Sal?
A: The varied topography means the best time can depend on your preference. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for cycling, with lush landscapes in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. Summer can be warm, especially on the plains, but higher elevations provide cooler air. Winter cycling is possible but may involve colder temperatures and potentially more challenging conditions.

Q: Is Poza de la Sal suitable for family touring cycling?
A: Yes, Poza de la Sal can be suitable for family touring cycling, especially on routes like the Vía Verde (Greenway), which offers gentle slopes and a safe, traffic-free environment. This allows families to enjoy the scenery and historical elements at a comfortable pace. Always check the difficulty rating and elevation profile of a specific route to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: How do other cyclists rate the touring routes in Poza de la Sal?
A: The touring cycling routes in Poza de la Sal are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an impressive average rating of 4.7 out of 5 stars from nearly 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from the unique salt flats to the challenging mountain views, and the opportunity to explore historical towns and natural monuments.

Q: Are there any routes that offer expansive views?
A: Many routes provide stunning panoramic views. The town of Poza de la Sal itself is strategically located, offering views of the La Bureba Plain and the Montes Obarenes. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31876418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de la Sal – Red Rock Valley loop from Poza de la Sal&lt;/a&gt; are known for their expansive vistas and challenging ascents that reward cyclists with breathtaking scenery.

Q: Where can I find places to rest or get refreshments along the routes?
A: Poza de la Sal and nearby historic towns like Oña offer various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can rest and refuel. When planning longer routes, it's advisable to check the route details for towns or villages along the way to ensure you have access to refreshments, especially in more remote sections.

Q: What is the difficulty level of the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes around Poza de la Sal cater to various skill levels. While there are some moderate options, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31483030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall Arch – Oña Historic Town Center loop from Poza de la Sal&lt;/a&gt;, the majority of touring cycling routes in the area are rated as difficult. These challenging routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Poza de la Sal – Town Hall Arch loop from Poza de la Sal&lt;/a&gt;, often feature significant elevation gains and longer distances, making them suitable for experienced touring cyclists.
tips: A tributary river of the Ebro River, it passes through the province of Burgos and is about 70 km long. It is born in the Montes de Oca, near Rábanos. The Oca River crosses Oña forming a narrow and beautiful gorge.

Villa declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in the Historic Site category in 1999. Do not leave Oña without discovering its architectural jewels: the Monastery of San Salvador, the parish church of San Juan Bautista, the Tower of San Juan and the building of the City hall.
Hacienda Sante is a beautiful palace located between Oña and Trespaderme, on the banks of the Oca River. In 2021, a businessman from Burgos bought Sante for private use.

The town of Poza de la Sal deserves a slightly longer visit. If you pass through here, do not hesitate to walk through its alleys and see its most emblematic places of interest: the medieval wall that surrounds the town and which is mostly preserved, and also its three gates (Puerta de las Eras, the Conjuradero, that of the City Hall), the church of San Cosme and San Damián, which is the heart of the urban area, the Administration House of the Reales Salinas and the City Hall building, among others. The name and fame of the town comes from its salt mines, which have been exploited since before Roman times.
